title: A vulnerability in MLflow's pyfunc extraction process allows for arbitrary file writes due to...
description: A vulnerability in MLflow's pyfunc extraction process allows for arbitrary file writes due to improper handling of tar archive entries. Specifically, the use of `tarfile.extractall` without path validation enables crafted tar.gz files containing `..` or absolute paths to escape the intended extraction directory. This issue affects the latest version of MLflow and poses a high/critical risk in scenarios involving multi-tenant environments or ingestion of untrusted artifacts, as it can lead to arb

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-15031.

title: Out-of-bounds array write in Xpdf 4.06 and earlier, due to incorrect validation of the "N" field...
description: Out-of-bounds array write in Xpdf 4.06 and earlier, due to incorrect validation of the "N" field in ICCBased color spaces.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows for an out-of-bounds array write, which could potentially lead to application crashes or, in rare cases, remote code execution. However, the severity is low due to limited exploitability and specific conditions required to trigger the issue.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to Xpdf version 4.07 or later, where the issue has been resolved.
Implement input validation and sandboxing for applications that process untrusted PDF files.
Monitor for updates or patches from vendors using Xpdf as a library in their software.

title: OmniGen2-RL contains an unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in the reward server...
description: OmniGen2-RL contains an unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in the reward server component that allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands by sending malicious HTTP POST requests. Attackers can exploit insecure pickle deserialization of request bodies to achieve code execution on the host system running the exposed service.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code on the host system, potentially leading to full system compromise, data breaches, service disruption, and lateral movement within the network.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the latest security patch or update provided by the OmniGen2-RL vendor to address CVE-2026-25873.
Implement network-level protections such as restricting access to the reward server component using firewalls or access control lists (ACLs).
Conduct a thorough review of exposed services and monitor for any signs of exploitation, such as unusual HTTP POST requests or unexpected system behavior.

title: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Customer Engagement (on-premises) 1612 (9.0.2.3034) allows the generation...
description: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Customer Engagement (on-premises) 1612 (9.0.2.3034) allows the generation of customized reports via raw SQL queries in an upload of a .rdl (Report Definition Language) file; this is then processed by the SQL Server Reporting Service. An account with the privilege Add Reporting Services Reports can upload a malicious rdl file. If the malicious rdl file is already loaded and it is executable by the user, the Add Reporting Services Reports privilege is not required. A malicio

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2025-58112.

title: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting') in nltk
description:
### Summary
`nltk.app.wordnet_app` contains a reflected cross-site scripting issue in the `lookup_...` route. A crafted `lookup_<payload>` URL can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript into the response page because attacker-controlled `word` data is reflected into HTML without escaping. This impacts users running the local WordNet Browser server and can lead to script execution in the browser origin of that application.

### Details
The vulnerable flow is in `nltk/app/wordnet_app.py`:

- [`nltk/app/

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an attacker to execute arbitrary JavaScript in the context of the user's browser when accessing the local WordNet Browser server. This could lead to session hijacking, data theft, or other malicious actions depending on the attacker's payload. However, the impact is limited to users running the local server, reducing the overall risk.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest version of nltk if a patch is available that addresses this vulnerability.
Restrict access to the local WordNet Browser server to trusted users and networks only.
Implement input sanitization and output encoding in the affected route to prevent improper neutralization of input.

title: Budibase Unrestricted Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) via REST Datasource Query Preview
description:
### Summary
The REST datasource query preview endpoint (`POST /api/queries/preview`) makes server-side HTTP requests to any URL supplied by the user in `fields.path` with no validation. An authenticated admin can reach internal services that are not exposed to the internet — including cloud metadata endpoints (AWS/GCP/Azure), internal databases, Kubernetes APIs, and other pods on the internal network. On GCP this leads to OAuth2 token theft with `cloud-platform` scope (full GCP access). On any d

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33226.

title: Nhost Storage Affected by MIME Type Spoofing via Trusted Client Content-Type Header in Storage Upload
description:
## Summary

The storage service's file upload handler trusts the client-provided `Content-Type` header without performing server-side MIME type detection. This allows an attacker to upload files with an arbitrary MIME type, bypassing any MIME-type-based restrictions configured on storage buckets.

## Affected Component

- **Service**: `services/storage`
- **File**: `services/storage/controller/upload_files.go`
- **Function**: `getMultipartFile` (lines 48-70)

## Root Cause

In `getMultipartFile`

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to spoof MIME types by manipulating the Content-Type header during file uploads. This can bypass MIME-type-based restrictions, potentially leading to unauthorized file uploads, execution of malicious scripts, or storage of harmful content. The impact is limited to systems relying solely on client-provided MIME type validation.
recommended_actions: Implement server-side MIME type validation to verify uploaded files' actual content type, regardless of the client-provided Content-Type header.
Apply stricter access controls and monitoring on storage buckets to detect and prevent unauthorized or suspicious file uploads.
Patch the affected component by updating the `getMultipartFile` function to include proper MIME type validation and sanitization.

title: Path traversal in Tekton Pipelines git resolver allows reading arbitrary files from the resolver pod
description:
### Summary

The Tekton Pipelines git resolver is vulnerable to path traversal via the `pathInRepo` parameter. A tenant with permission to create `ResolutionRequests` (e.g. by creating `TaskRuns` or `PipelineRuns` that use the git resolver) can read arbitrary files from the resolver pod's filesystem, including ServiceAccount tokens. The file contents are returned base64-encoded in `resolutionrequest.status.data`.

### Details

The git resolver's `getFileContent()` function in `pkg/resolution/res

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33211.

title: JustHTML has a Sanitizer Bypass (in Markdown)
description:
## Summary

`to_markdown()` does not sufficiently escape text content that looks like HTML. As a result, untrusted input that is safe in `to_html()` can become raw HTML in Markdown output.

This is not specific to tokenizer raw-text states like `<title>`, `<noscript>`, or `<plaintext>`, although those states can trigger the behavior. The root cause is broader: Markdown text serialization leaves angle brackets unescaped in text nodes.

## Details

When converting a parsed document to Markdown, te

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: JustHTML Affected by Mutation XSS via Literal Text Serialization in Raw Text Elements (style/script)
description:
## Summary

Sanitized DOM trees can be unsafe to serialize when a custom policy allows raw-text elements such as `<style>` or `<script>`.

The issue affects DOM trees that are constructed or modified programmatically and then passed through `sanitize_dom()` with a policy that keeps these elements. Text nodes inside `<style>` and `<script>` are serialized literally, so attacker-controlled text containing the matching closing tag sequence can break out of the raw-text context and inject HTML into

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to inject malicious HTML or JavaScript by exploiting improperly sanitized raw-text elements such as <style> or <script>. This could lead to cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks, compromising the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of web applications.
recommended_actions: Review and update the `sanitize_dom()` policy to disallow raw-text elements like <style> and <script> or ensure proper escaping of text nodes within these elements.
Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers to mitigate the impact of potential XSS attacks.
Conduct a thorough security audit of applications that programmatically construct or modify DOM trees to identify and address similar vulnerabilities.

title: Unsigned SAML LogoutRequest Acceptance in gosaml2
description:
## Summary

The `ValidateEncodedLogoutRequestPOST` function in gosaml2 accepts completely unsigned SAML `LogoutRequest` messages even when `SkipSignatureValidation` is set to `false`. When `validateElementSignature` returns `dsig.ErrMissingSignature`, the code in `decode_logout_request.go:60-62` silently falls through to process the unverified XML element instead of rejecting it. An attacker who can reach the SP's Single Logout endpoint can forge a `LogoutRequest` for any user, terminating their

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: gosaml2 CBC Padding Panic — Unauthenticated Process Crash
description:
## Summary

The AES-CBC decryption path in `DecryptBytes()` panics on crafted ciphertext whose plaintext is all zero bytes. After decryption, `bytes.TrimRight(data, "\x00")` empties the slice, then `data[len(data)-1]` panics with `index out of range [-1]`. There is no `recover()` in the library. The panic propagates through `ValidateEncodedResponse` and kills the goroutine (or the entire process in non-`net/http` servers). An attacker needs only the SP's public RSA key (published in SAML metadat

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an attacker to crash the application by exploiting a flaw in the AES-CBC decryption process. This can lead to denial-of-service (DoS) conditions, affecting system availability and potentially disrupting critical operations.
recommended_actions: Update the gosaml2 library to the latest patched version as soon as it becomes available to address the vulnerability.
Implement input validation and error handling mechanisms to prevent crafted ciphertext from causing application crashes.
Monitor application logs for signs of exploitation attempts and establish alerts for unusual activity.

title: validateSignature Loop Variable Capture Signature Bypass in goxmldsig
description:
### Details

The `validateSignature` function in `validate.go` goes through the references in the `SignedInfo` block to find one that matches the signed element's ID. In Go versions before 1.22, or when `go.mod` uses an older version, there is a loop variable capture issue. The code takes the address of the loop variable `_ref` instead of its value. As a result, if more than one reference matches the ID or if the loop logic is incorrect, the `ref` pointer will always end up pointing to the last

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Natural Language Toolkit (NLTK) has unbounded recursion in JSONTaggedDecoder.decode_obj() may cause DoS

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: mo has a XSS via inline SVG script tags in Markdown rendering
description:
### Summary

When rendering Markdown files containing inline SVG elements with `<script>` tags, the embedded JavaScript is executed in the browser. This is due to `rehype-raw` passing raw HTML (including SVG) through to the DOM without sanitization.

### PoC

```html
<svg><script>alert(1)</script></svg>
```

Embedding the above in a Markdown file opened with mo causes the script to execute.

### Impact

Arbitrary JavaScript execution in the context of the mo application when a user opens a malic

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows for arbitrary JavaScript execution via inline SVG script tags in Markdown files. This could lead to cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks, potentially compromising user data, session tokens, or enabling further exploitation within the application context.
recommended_actions: Implement proper sanitization of Markdown content by disabling or filtering out potentially dangerous HTML and SVG elements, such as <script> tags, before rendering.
Update the Markdown rendering pipeline to use a secure library or configuration that prevents raw HTML from being passed to the DOM (e.g., configure 'rehype-raw' to sanitize inputs).
Educate users about the risks of opening untrusted Markdown files and encourage safe file handling practices.

title: SimpleJWT has an Unauthenticated Denial of Service via JWE header tampering
description:
## Summary

An unauthenticated attacker can perform a Denial of Service via JWE header tampering when PBES2 algorithms are used.  
Applications that call `JWE::decrypt()` on attacker-controlled JWEs using PBES2 algorithms are affected.

## Details

PHP version: `PHP 8.4.11`
SimpleJWT version: `v1.1.0`

The relevant portion of the vulnerable implementation is shown below ([PBES2.php](https://github.com/kelvinmo/simplejwt/blob/edb7807a240b72c59e72d7dca31add9d16555f9f/src/SimpleJWT/Crypt/KeyManagem

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33204.

title:
CVE-2025-15038 - An Out-of-Bounds
Read vulnerability exists in the ASUS Business System
Control Interface driver. Thi
description:
An Out-of-Bounds
Read vulnerability exists in the ASUS Business System
Control Interface driver. This vulnerability can be triggered by an unprivileged local  user
sending a specially crafted IOCTL  request, potentially leading
to a disclosure of
kernel information or a system crash. Refer to the "Security Update for ASUS 
Business System Control Interface" section on the ASUS Security Advisory for more information.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an unprivileged local user to exploit the ASUS Business System Control Interface driver, potentially leading to kernel information disclosure or a system crash. This could result in sensitive information leakage or denial of service on affected systems.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patch provided in the ASUS Security Advisory for the Business System Control Interface driver.
Restrict local user access to systems running the vulnerable driver until the patch is applied.
Monitor systems for unusual IOCTL requests and implement endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title:
CVE-2025-15037 - An Incorrect
Permission Assignment vulnerability exists in the ASUS Business
System Control Interfac
description:
An Incorrect
Permission Assignment vulnerability exists in the ASUS Business
System Control Interface driver. This vulnerability can be triggered by an unprivileged local user sending a
specially crafted IOCTL request,
potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive hardware resources
and kernel information disclosure. Refer to the "ASUS Business System Control Interface" section on the ASUS Security Advisory for more information.

description: A vulnerability was detected in 648540858 wvp-GB28181-pro up to 2.7.4-20260107. title: CVE-2026-3965 - A security vulnerability has been detected in whyour qinglong up to 2.20.1. Affected is an unknown f
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in whyour qinglong up to 2.20.1. Affected is an unknown function of the file back/loaders/express.ts of the component API Interface. The manipulation of the argument command leads to protection mechanism failure. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. Upgrading to version 2.20.2 is able to address this issue. The identifier of the patch is 6bec52dca158481258315ba0fc2f11206df7b719. It is advisable to upgrade the affected component. The code maintainer was informed beforehand about the issues. He reacted very fast and highly professional.
